title: Assign a distinct role per cache layer
description: Give browser, CDN, application, and DB-buffer tiers separate responsibilities and freshness budgets.
body: Use when one cache tier cannot satisfy both latency and freshness. Do not replicate identical TTLs across browser, CDN, and application tiers — uncoordinated layers compound staleness and make invalidation ambiguous. Actions: assign each layer a role (browser for per-user, CDN for shared edge content, app/Redis for computed values, DB buffer for rows) and set the shortest freshness closest to the source of truth. Validation signals include a documented end-to-end worst-case staleness equal to the sum of un-purgeable layer TTLs. Risk boundary: invalidating only one layer leaves the others stale — escalate if a layer cannot be purged on demand.
